metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `land-contract` to the AC1 grep-clean pattern so the acceptance check matches the full sweep described in the What section NOTE.
findings.finding: AC1's grep-clean verification omits `land-contract`, which the What section explicitly lists as a target of the removal sweep ('grep sweep the hyphenated forms: `autolander`, `land-contract`, `land-status`'). The acceptance criterion says only '(grep clean for `infra/autolander` and `land-status`)', leaving no machine-verifiable check that `land-contract` references were fully excised from code/config.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC1 vs. What section NOTE (grep sweep clause)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to read: '(grep clean for `infra/autolander`, `land-contract`, and `land-status`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow AC1's grep-clean scope to exclude `infra/` subdirectories that are bcf0's territory (e.g., scope to `src/`, `tests/`, `scripts/`, top-level config, `docs/`), or add a note that AC1 is fully satisfied only after bcf0 lands.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts grep-clean for 'infra/autolander' across 'code/tests/config', but infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh (line 62: AUTOLANDER_PATHS='infra/autolander/ ...') and other infra/ files (infra/compose/docker-compose.yml, infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh) all contain 'infra/autolander' and are deferred to sibling bcf0. Until bcf0 lands, running AC1's grep across the whole repo will return hits and appear to fail. The AC does not scope its grep to exclude infra/ directories owned by bcf0, creating an ambiguous verification condition for this ticket alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'No references to the `infra/autolander` package path... remain in code/tests/config (grep clean for `infra/autolander` and `land-status`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to: 'No references to the `infra/autolander` package path or the `land`/`land-status` command remain in `src/`, `tests/`, top-level config files, or `docs/` (grep clean for `infra/autolander` and `land-status` scoped to those directories; `infra/` consumers are resolved by sibling bcf0).'

findings.finding: AC1 states 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)' — but after deleting only the code scope the plan describes, confirmed-existing references in infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/terraform/ssm.tf, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/compose-up.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh, infra/gerrit/project.config, and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md would still be present. ADR 0047 scopes these to separate work items (bcf0, 5e7a), but the AC as written makes no such exclusion and would evaluate as failing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC1 with: 'No references to infra/autolander/, tests/autolander/, or the land/land-status commands remain in src/, pyproject.toml, or any file not covered by sibling work items bcf0 (infra decommission) and 5e7a (docs sweep).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1 (or add AC1b) to verify `land-contract` and standalone `autolander` are also grep-clean in code/config — e.g. state the default: `grep -rE '(land-contract|\bautolander\b)' --include='*.py' --include='*.cfg' --include='*.toml' --include='*.yml' infra/ src/ tests/ exits non-zero (no matches)`.
findings.finding: AC1 verifies `infra/autolander` and `land-status` are grep-clean, but the sweep step also removes `land-contract` and standalone `autolander` references from code/config. No acceptance criterion proves those two patterns are gone after the sweep, leaving the step unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; What — third bullet (grep sweep)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: `- [ ] No bare references to 'land-contract' or 'autolander' remain in code/tests/config (grep -rE '(land-contract|\bautolander\b)' --include='*.py' --include='*.toml' --include='*.yml' --include='*.cfg' . exits non-zero outside docs/ and CHANGELOG).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `land-contract` to AC1's grep-clean patterns so the acceptance criterion covers all three terms named in the What-section sweep.
findings.finding: The plan lists three grep-sweep terms in the What section ('autolander', 'land-contract', 'land-status') and correctly uses hyphenated forms. However, AC1 only verifies two of those three patterns ('infra/autolander' and 'land-status') and omits 'land-contract' from the grep-clean assertion. After deleting docs/land-contract.md, dangling references to 'land-contract' remain in CONTRIBUTING.md (×2), README.md (×1), and CLAUDE.md (×1) — all deferred to 5e7a — but no AC verifies that the code/tests/config files themselves are clean of 'land-contract' references beyond the deleted infra/autolander/ code.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 vs. What section bullet 3
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 to: '...grep clean for `infra/autolander`, `land-status`, and `land-contract` (scoped to code/tests/config excluding bcf0-owned infra/ paths and top-level prose deferred to 5e7a).'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out note (e.g. 'Revert via `git revert <SHA>`; sibling stories bcf0/5e7a must be coordinated if this story is rolled back') so the destructive step satisfies T4's reversibility requirement.
findings.finding: The plan performs an irreversible deletion (no undo short of `git revert`) but provides no explicit back-out plan or rollback note. T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not merely justification for the destruction.
findings.location: Plan overall — no rollback/back-out section exists
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line 'Rollback' note under Scope: 'If roll-back is needed before bcf0/5e7a land, `git revert` this commit; coordinate with bcf0 to avoid deployed-infra drift.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update docs/adr/0042 body (e.g., add a 'Status: Superseded / Retired' header note and a brief rationale sentence) so the ADR records that the land-contract architecture was removed in this change, not just de-linked.
findings.finding: docs/adr/0042 documents the land-contract architectural decision. The plan de-links the two `../land-contract.md` hrefs within it but does not update or retire the ADR body to record that the architecture it describes has been removed. After this change, a reader of ADR 0042 will encounter an architectural narrative describing an active land-contract/autolander pattern with no indication it was retired — a stale architectural record.
findings.location: What section / 'Fix the docs/-internal links ... de-link the two `../land-contract.md` references in docs/adr/0042'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a status line near the top of docs/adr/0042 such as: `**Status: Retired** — The autolander bot and land-contract were removed (story <this ticket>); this ADR is preserved for historical reference only.`

findings.finding: The grep sweep pattern listed in the What section is 'land_contract' (underscore), but every actual reference to the deleted document in the repo uses 'land-contract' (hyphen) — in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, and elsewhere. A grep for 'land_contract' returns zero hits and will silently pass even when broken doc links to the deleted docs/land-contract.md remain, undermining the plan's own sweep-based verification mechanism.
findings.location: What section — 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the grep sweep term to 'land-contract' (hyphen) to match the actual artifact name. Since broad doc updates are scoped to story 5e7a, it is acceptable to note that grep-for-'land-contract' hits in top-level contributor docs (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md) are tracked under 5e7a and intentionally left for that story — but the sweep term must use the correct form so the implementer can make that judgment consciously rather than missing the hits entirely.

findings.finding: The plan's grep sweep term `land_contract` (underscore) does not match the actual identifier used in all live references, which use `land-contract` (hyphen). A sweep using only the underscore form will miss references in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, docs/adr/, and infra/autolander/land.py.
findings.location: What section: 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the What bullet to: 'grep sweep: autolander, land-contract, land-status'

findings.finding: The grep sweep terms listed (`autolander`, `land_contract`, `land-status`) use underscore/exact forms but omit the hyphenated form `land-contract`, which is the exact filename of the deleted doc and the likely link text in README, CONTRIBUTING, or other markdown files. References of the form `[land-contract](docs/land-contract.md)` or `docs/land-contract.md` would not be caught, leaving stale doc links after deletion.
findings.location: What section — grep sweep: 'autolander, land_contract, land-status'; also AC 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `land-contract` and `land contract` to the grep sweep command, e.g.: `grep -r 'autolander\|land_contract\|land-status\|land-contract\|land contract' --include='*.md' --include='*.py' --include='*.toml' --include='*.cfg' .`

findings.finding: The grep sweep terms listed are 'autolander, land_contract, land-status' (underscore form), but the deleted doc is named 'land-contract.md' (hyphen). Other documentation files (READMEs, CHANGELOG, contribution guides, wiki pages) that reference the hyphenated forms 'land-contract' or 'land command' could be missed by the sweep, stranding stale references.
findings.location: What section — 'Remove any imports/registry entries/refs to the above (grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the grep sweep: `grep -r 'land.contract\|land.status\|land command\|autolander' docs/ *.md` (or equivalent) alongside the code sweep, and confirm zero hits before closing.

findings.finding: The plan's grep sweep term is written as `land_contract` (underscore), but every actual reference to the contract document in the repo uses the hyphenated form `land-contract` — confirmed at README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161, CONTRIBUTING.md:537, CLAUDE.md:595, docs/README.md:108, docs/adr/0042-auto-lander.md:11 and :77, docs/adr/0047-retire-autolander-rebase-if-necessary.md:127 and :131. The underscore form appears only inside cached plan-review JSON artifacts. A sweep on `land_contract` will match none of these live references and produce a false-clean result.
findings.location: What section — 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the sweep pattern to `land-contract` (hyphen). Also add explicit update steps for README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161 and :537, CLAUDE.md:595, and docs/README.md:108, which all link to docs/land-contract.md and must be updated or have the link removed when the file is deleted.

findings.finding: The grep sweep pattern 'land_contract' (underscore) is the wrong identifier and will miss every live dangling reference to the deleted 'docs/land-contract.md'. All actual references in the codebase use the hyphenated form 'land-contract' (README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161, CLAUDE.md:595, docs/README.md:108, docs/adr/0042-auto-lander.md). After deletion these become broken links, but the grep-clean AC passes trivially on the underscore term, giving a false-negative result and allowing the CI suite to fail later (docs index test, link checker).
findings.location: What section — grep sweep list: 'autolander, land_contract, land-status'; AC line 1 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `land_contract` with `land-contract` in the What grep sweep list and AC1. The full sweep command would be: `grep -r 'land-contract\|land_contract\|autolander\|land-status' . --include='*.md' --include='*.py' --include='*.sh' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.tf' | grep -v '.rebar/cache'`

findings.finding: The grep-clean AC names search terms (autolander, land_contract, land-status) but does not specify a concrete anchored grep command, leaving the proving command susceptible to substring false-positives and unclear directory scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'

findings.finding: The plan's grep sweep term 'land_contract' (underscore) will not match any real reference in the repo — every occurrence uses the hyphenated form 'land-contract' (the filename is docs/land-contract.md). AC1's grep-clean check would silently pass while live references remain in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, and docs/adr/ files.
findings.location: What section — grep sweep: 'autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `land_contract` with `land-contract` in the grep sweep list: `grep -r 'autolander\|land-contract\|land-status' .`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
